ansi(3) Library Functions Manual ansi(3)

NAME

ansi - ANSI Utilities

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ansi_rle_context_t
struct ansi_timing_t
struct rgb_error_t

Enumerations


enum ansi_color_mode_t { ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0, ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND }

Functions


char * ansi_strip_escapes (const char *input, size_t input_len)
Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char *message, size_t pos)
Check if a position in text is already colored. char * append_truecolor_fg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_bg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_fg_bg (char *dst, uint8_t fg_r, uint8_t fg_g, uint8_t fg_b, uint8_t bg_r, uint8_t bg_g, uint8_t bg_b)
void ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, char *buffer, size_t capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)
void ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)
void ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx)
void ansi_fast_init (void)
void ansi_fast_init_256color (void)
void ansi_fast_init_16color (void)
char * append_256color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_256color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_16color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_16color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
void get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t *r, uint8_t *g, uint8_t *b)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t *error_buffer)
char * append_color_fg_for_mode (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)

Detailed Description

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum ansi_color_mode_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>

Enumerator

ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND
ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND
ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND

Definition at line 36 of file ansi.h.

36              {
37   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0,
38   ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
39   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
40 } ansi_color_mode_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  ansi_fast_init (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize fast
    ANSI generation (call once at startup)


Definition at line 301 of file ansi.c.


301                           {
302   // Initialize the dec3 cache used by truecolor functions
303   ascii_simd_init();
304 }

    mirror_init_with_args(), and session_display_create().






void
  ansi_fast_init_16color (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize
    16-color mode (thread-safe, called on first use)


Definition at line 380 of file ansi.c.


380                                   {
381   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_color16_lc, "ansi_16color")) {
382     return; // Already initialized
383   }
384   do_init_16color();
385 }

Referenced by append_16color_bg(),

    image_print_16color_dithered(),
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(),
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar(), and
    session_display_create().

bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char * message,
  size_t pos)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Check if a position in text is already colored. Scans from the
    start of the message to the given position, tracking ANSI escape codes.
    Returns true if there is an active color code (not in reset state).




Detects reset codes: \x1b[0m and \x1b[m




Parameters


message The full message


pos Position to check


Returns


true if already colored (not in reset state), false if in
  reset state


Definition at line 74 of file ansi.c.


74                                                                 {
75   if (!message) {
76     return false;
77   }
78 
79   bool in_reset = true; // Start in reset state
80 
81   // Scan from beginning to current position looking for ANSI codes
82   for (size_t i = 0; i < pos && message[i] != '\0'; i++) {
83     if (message[i] == '\x1b' && message[i + 1] == '[') {
84       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
85       // Look for the end marker 'm'
86       size_t j = i + 2;
87       while (message[j] != '\0' && message[j] != 'm') {
88         j++;
89       }
90 
91       if (message[j] == 'm') {
92         // Extract the color code part (between [ and m)
93         // Check if it's a reset code: \x1b[0m or \x1b[m
94         if ((j == i + 3 && message[i + 2] == '0') || (j == i + 2)) {
95           // Reset code
96           in_reset = true;
97         } else {
98           // Color code (anything else)
99           in_reset = false;
100         }
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   // Return true if NOT in reset state (already colorized)
106   return !in_reset;
107 }






Referenced by colorize_log_message().






void
  ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, uint8_t r,
  uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Add pixel with
    run-length encoding (only emits SGR when color changes)


Parameters


ctx RLE context


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


ascii_char ASCII character to append


Definition at line 243 of file ansi.c.


243                                                                                                    {
244   // Check if we need to emit a new SGR sequence
245   bool color_changed = ctx->first_pixel || (r != ctx->last_r) || (g != ctx->last_g) || (b != ctx->last_b);
246 
247   if (color_changed) {
248     if (ctx->length + 40 >= ctx->capacity) {
249       // Log when we can't add a color change due to buffer capacity
250       log_warn_every(US_PER_SEC_INT, "RLE_CAPACITY: Cannot add SGR at length=%zu, capacity=%zu (need 40 bytes)",
251                      ctx->length, ctx->capacity);
252     }
253   }
254 
255   if (color_changed && (ctx->length + 40 < ctx->capacity)) { // Reserve 40 bytes for SGR (FG+BG max is 38)
256     char *pos = ctx->buffer + ctx->length;
257 
258     switch (ctx->mode) {
259     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND:
260       pos = append_truecolor_fg(pos, r, g, b);
261       break;
262     case ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND:
263       pos = append_truecolor_bg(pos, r, g, b);
264       break;
265     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND:
266       // For FG+BG mode, use a default background (black) or implement dual-color logic
267       pos = append_truecolor_fg_bg(pos, r, g, b, 0, 0, 0);
268       break;
269     }
270 
271     ctx->length = (size_t)(pos - ctx->buffer);
272     ctx->last_r = r;
273     ctx->last_g = g;
274     ctx->last_b = b;
275     ctx->first_pixel = false;
276   }
277 
278   // Add the ASCII character
279   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity - 1) {
280     ctx->buffer[ctx->length++] = ascii_char;
281   }
282 }






References ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
    append_truecolor_bg(), append_truecolor_fg(),
    append_truecolor_fg_bg(), b,
    ansi_rle_context_t::buffer, ansi_rle_context_t::capacity,
    ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel, ansi_rle_context_t::last_b,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_g, ansi_rle_context_t::last_r,
    ansi_rle_context_t::length, log_warn_every,
    ansi_rle_context_t::mode, and US_PER_SEC_INT.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Finish RLE
    sequence with reset and null terminator


Parameters


ctx RLE context


Definition at line 285 of file ansi.c.


285                                               {
286   // Add reset sequence (requires 4 bytes + 1 for null terminator = 5 bytes total)
287   if (ctx->length + 5 <= ctx->capacity) {
288     SAFE_MEMCPY(ctx->buffer + ctx->length, 4, "\033[0m", 4);
289     ctx->length += 4;
290   }
291 
292   // Null terminate
293   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity) {
294     ctx->buffer[ctx->length] = '\0';
295   }
296 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    SAFE_MEMCPY.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, char * buffer, size_t
  capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize RLE
    context for efficient ANSI generation


Parameters


ctx Context to initialize


buffer Output buffer


capacity Buffer capacity


mode Color mode (foreground/background/both)


Definition at line 230 of file ansi.c.


230                                                                                                    {
231   ctx->buffer = buffer;
232   ctx->capacity = capacity;
233   ctx->length = 0;
234   ctx->mode = mode;
235   ctx->first_pixel = true;
236   // Initialize with impossible color values to force first SGR
237   ctx->last_r = 0xFF;
238   ctx->last_g = 0xFF;
239   ctx->last_b = 0xFF;
240 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_b, ansi_rle_context_t::last_g,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_r, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    ansi_rle_context_t::mode.


Referenced by image_print_color().






char
  * ansi_strip_escapes (const char * input, size_t input_len)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. Removes all ANSI
    CSI sequences (ESC [ ... final_byte) from the input, leaving only printable
    text. Useful for creating plain text output from colorized ASCII art.




Parameters


input Input string containing ANSI escape
  sequences


input_len Length of input string


Returns


Newly allocated string with escapes removed (caller must
  free), or NULL on error


Definition at line 23 of file ansi.c.


23                                                               {
24   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
25     return NULL;
26   }
27 
28   // Allocate buffer directly for output (at most input_len bytes)
29   char *output = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len + 1, char *);
30   if (!output) {
31     return NULL;
32   }
33 
34   size_t out_pos = 0;
35   size_t i = 0;
36 
37   while (i < input_len) {
38     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
39     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
40       // Skip ESC[
41       i += 2;
42 
43       // Skip parameter bytes (digits, semicolons, and intermediate bytes)
44       while (i < input_len) {
45         char c = input[i];
46         // Parameter bytes: 0x30-0x3F (digits, semicolon, etc.)
47         // Intermediate bytes: 0x20-0x2F (space, !, ", etc.)
48         if ((c >= 0x30 && c <= 0x3F) || (c >= 0x20 && c <= 0x2F)) {
49           i++;
50         } else {
51           break;
52         }
53       }
54 
55       // Skip final byte (0x40-0x7E: @, A-Z, [, \, ], ^, _, `, a-z, {, |, }, ~)
56       if (i < input_len) {
57         char final = input[i];
58         if (final >= 0x40 && final <= 0x7E) {
59           i++;
60         }
61       }
62     } else {
63       // Regular character - copy to output
64       output[out_pos++] = input[i];
65       i++;
66     }
67   }
68 
69   // Null-terminate the output
70   output[out_pos] = '\0';
71   return output;
72 }






References SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by display_height(), display_width(),
    layout_print_two_column_row(), and log_mmap_write().






char
  * append_16color_bg (char * dst, uint8_t color_index)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append 16-color
    ANSI background SGR sequence


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 403 of file ansi.c.


403                                                         {
404   // Ensure initialization before use
405   ansi_fast_init_16color();
406 
407   if (color_index >= 16) {
408     color_index = 0; // Default to black background
409   }
410 
411   const char *color_str = color16_bg_strings[color_index];
412   while (*color_str) {
413     *dst++ = *color_str++;
414   }
415 
416   return dst;
417 }






References ansi_fast_init_16color().


Referenced by
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().

char
  * append_color_fg_for_mode (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append color SGR
    sequence for the appropriate terminal color mode


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


mode Terminal color mode capability


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 570 of file ansi.c.


570                                                                                                        {
571   switch (mode) {
572   case COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR:
573     return append_truecolor_fg(dst, r, g, b);
574 
575   case COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR: {
576     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(r, g, b);
577     return append_256color_fg(dst, color_index);
578   }
579 
580   case COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR: {
581     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(r, g, b);
582     return append_16color_fg(dst, color_index);
583   }
584 
585   case COLOR_MODE_NONE:
586   case COLOR_MODE_AUTO:
587   default:
588     // No color output for monochrome mode or auto mode (fallback)
589     return dst;
590   }
591 }






References append_16color_fg(),
    append_256color_fg(), append_truecolor_fg(), b,
    COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR, COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR,
    COLOR_MODE_AUTO, COLOR_MODE_NONE, COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR,
    rgb_to_16color(), and rgb_to_256color().






char
  * append_truecolor_bg (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append truecolor
    background SGR sequence Maximum output: 19 bytes
    (\033[48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 155 of file ansi.c.


155                                                                       {
156   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
157   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
158     init_dec3();
159   }
160 
161   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[48;2;", 7);
162   dst += 7;
163 
164   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
165   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
166   *dst++ = ';';
167 
168   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
169   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
170   *dst++ = ';';
171 
172   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
173   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
174   *dst++ = 'm';
175 
176   return dst;
177 }

    SAFE_MEMCPY.

  * append_truecolor_fg (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,

Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 125 of file ansi.c.


125                                                                       {
126   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
127   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
128     init_dec3();
129   }
130 
131   // Static prefix - 7 bytes
132   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[38;2;", 7);
133   dst += 7;
134 
135   // Red component + semicolon
136   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
137   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
138   *dst++ = ';';
139 
140   // Green component + semicolon
141   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
142   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
143   *dst++ = ';';
144 
145   // Blue component + suffix
146   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
147   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
148   *dst++ = 'm';
149 
150   return dst;
151 }

  fg_g, uint8_t fg_b, uint8_t bg_r, uint8_t bg_g,
  uint8_t bg_b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append combined
    foreground + background truecolor SGR Maximum output: 38 bytes
    (\033[38;2;255;255;255;48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


fg_r Foreground red component


fg_g Foreground green component


fg_b Foreground blue component


bg_r Background red component


bg_g Background green component


bg_b Background blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 181 of file ansi.c.


182                                            {
183   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
184   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
185     init_dec3();
186   }
187 
188   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 38, "\033[38;2;", 7);
189   dst += 7;
190 
191   // Foreground RGB (remaining: 31 bytes max)
192   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 31, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len);
193   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len;
194   *dst++ = ';';
195 
196   // Remaining: 27 bytes max
197   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 27, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len);
198   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len;
199   *dst++ = ';';
200 
201   // Remaining: 23 bytes max
202   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 23, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len);
203   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len;
204 
205   // Background RGB (remaining: 20 bytes max)
206   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 20, ";48;2;", 6);
207   dst += 6;
208 
209   // Remaining: 14 bytes max
210   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 14, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len);
211   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len;
212   *dst++ = ';';
213 
214   // Remaining: 10 bytes max
215   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 10, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].len);
216   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].len;
217   *dst++ = ';';
218 
219   // Remaining: 6 bytes max
220   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 6, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].len);
221   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].len;
222   *dst++ = 'm';
223

225 }

void
  get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t * r,
  uint8_t * g, uint8_t * b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Get RGB values for
    a 16-color ANSI index


Parameters


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


r Output red component


g Output green component


b Output blue component


Definition at line 462 of file ansi.c.


462                                                                               {
463   // Same color table as rgb_to_16color()
464   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
465       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
466       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
467       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
468       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
469       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
470       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
471       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
472       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
473       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
474       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
475       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
476       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
477       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
478       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
479       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
480       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
481   };
482 
483   if (color_index >= 16) {
484     color_index = 7; // Default to light gray
485   }
486 
487   *r = ansi_colors[color_index][0];
488   *g = ansi_colors[color_index][1];
489   *b = ansi_colors[color_index][2];
490 }






References b.


Referenced by
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and
    rgb_to_16color_dithered().






uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 16-color ANSI index Uses distance-based matching against standard
    ANSI colors


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


16-color index (0-15)


Definition at line 419 of file ansi.c.


419                                                         {
420   // Convert RGB to the closest 16-color ANSI color
421   // This uses a simple distance-based approach
422 
423   // Define the 16 ANSI colors in RGB
424   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
425       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
426       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
427       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
428       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
429       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
430       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
431       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
432       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
433       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
434       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
435       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
436       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
437       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
438       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
439       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
440       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
441   };
442 
443   uint8_t best_match = 0;
444   int min_distance = INT_MAX;
445 
446   for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
447     int dr = (int)r - (int)ansi_colors[i][0];
448     int dg = (int)g - (int)ansi_colors[i][1];
449     int db = (int)b - (int)ansi_colors[i][2];
450     int distance = dr * dr + dg * dg + db * db;
451 
452     if (distance < min_distance) {
453       min_distance = distance;
454       best_match = i;
455     }
456   }
457 
458   return best_match;
459 }






References b.


Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), image_print_16color(),
    rgb_to_16color_dithered(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().






uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int
  x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t * error_buffer)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    16-color with Floyd-Steinberg dithering


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


x Current pixel X coordinate


y Current pixel Y coordinate


width Image width


height Image height


error_buffer Dithering error buffer (width*height elements)


Returns


16-color index with dithering applied


Definition at line 493 of file ansi.c.


493                                                                                                                      {
494   // Add accumulated error from previous pixels
495   if (error_buffer) {
496     // Use size_t for index calculation to prevent integer overflow on large images
497     size_t error_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
498     r += error_buffer[error_idx].r;
499     g += error_buffer[error_idx].g;
500     b += error_buffer[error_idx].b;
501 
502     // Reset error for this pixel
503     error_buffer[error_idx].r = 0;
504     error_buffer[error_idx].g = 0;
505     error_buffer[error_idx].b = 0;
506   }
507 
508   // Clamp values to [0, 255]
509   uint8_t r_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)r);
510   uint8_t g_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)g);
511   uint8_t b_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)b);
512 
513   // Find the closest 16-color match
514   uint8_t closest_color = rgb_to_16color(r_clamped, g_clamped, b_clamped);
515 
516   // Calculate quantization error if dithering is enabled
517   if (error_buffer) {
518     uint8_t actual_r, actual_g, actual_b;
519     get_16color_rgb(closest_color, &actual_r, &actual_g, &actual_b);
520 
521     int error_r = r - (int)actual_r;
522     int error_g = g - (int)actual_g;
523     int error_b = b - (int)actual_b;
524 
525     // Distribute error using Floyd-Steinberg weights:
526     //     * 7/16
527     // 3/16 5/16 1/16
528 
529     // Error to right pixel (x+1, y)
530     // Use size_t for all index calculations to prevent integer overflow
531     if (x + 1 < width) {
532       size_t right_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
533       error_buffer[right_idx].r += (error_r * 7) / 16;
534       error_buffer[right_idx].g += (error_g * 7) / 16;
535       error_buffer[right_idx].b += (error_b * 7) / 16;
536     }
537 
538     // Error to pixels on next row (y+1)
539     if (y + 1 < height) {
540       // Bottom-left pixel (x-1, y+1)
541       if (x - 1 >= 0) {
542         size_t bl_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x - 1);
543         error_buffer[bl_idx].r += (error_r * 3) / 16;
544         error_buffer[bl_idx].g += (error_g * 3) / 16;
545         error_buffer[bl_idx].b += (error_b * 3) / 16;
546       }
547 
548       // Bottom pixel (x, y+1)
549       size_t bottom_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
550       error_buffer[bottom_idx].r += (error_r * 5) / 16;
551       error_buffer[bottom_idx].g += (error_g * 5) / 16;
552       error_buffer[bottom_idx].b += (error_b * 5) / 16;
553 
554       // Bottom-right pixel (x+1, y+1)
555       if (x + 1 < width) {
556         size_t br_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
557         error_buffer[br_idx].r += (error_r * 1) / 16;
558         error_buffer[br_idx].g += (error_g * 1) / 16;
559         error_buffer[br_idx].b += (error_b * 1) / 16;
560       }
561     }
562   }
563 
564   return closest_color;
565 }






References b, rgb_error_t::b, rgb_error_t::g,
    get_16color_rgb(), rgb_error_t::r, and
    rgb_to_16color().


Referenced by image_print_16color_dithered(), and
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background().






uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 256-color palette index Uses 6x6x6 color cube + grayscale
  ramp


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


256-color palette index (0-255)


Definition at line 342 of file ansi.c.


342                                                          {
343   // Map to 6x6x6 color cube (216 colors) + grayscale ramp
344 
345   // Check if it's close to grayscale
346   int avg = (r + g + b) / 3;
347   int gray_diff = abs(r - avg) + abs(g - avg) + abs(b - avg);
348 
349   if (gray_diff < 30) {
350     // Use grayscale ramp (colors 232-255)
351     int gray_level = (avg * 23) / 255;
352     return (uint8_t)(232 + gray_level);
353   }
354 
355   // Use 6x6x6 color cube (colors 16-231)
356   int r6 = (r * 5) / 255;
357   int g6 = (g * 5) / 255;
358   int b6 = (b * 5) / 255;
359 
360   return (uint8_t)(16 + (r6 * 36) + (g6 * 6) + b6);
361 }






References b.


Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), and
    rgb_to_256color_halfblocks_scalar().
